skill.name: rollinggo-searchhotel
skill.description: Use the RollingGo CLI to search hotels, filter results, read hotel tags, and retrieve hotel room prices.
skill.summary: Search hotels and room prices through the RollingGo CLI using destination, date, budget, star rating, and tag filters.
skill.author: RollingGo-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly detected external command examples, API key handling, network references, and host environment configuration. Review found no prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent, but the skill should publish with a warning because it directs agents to run latest third-party CLI packages and handle RollingGo_API_KEY.

medium_findings.title: Runtime Execution of Latest Third-Party CLI Packages
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to execute rollinggo@latest through npx or uvx. This is legitimate for the hotel workflow, but it creates supply-chain risk because the package resolved at runtime can change after review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution instructions are explicit and semantically required by the skill. The risk is supply-chain exposure from latest package resolution, not evidence of malicious code in the skill files.

medium_findings.title: API Key Handling Through Environment and CLI Arguments
medium_findings.description: The skill requires RollingGo_API_KEY and documents passing it through environment variables or the --api-key option. This is expected for an authenticated API, but secrets can leak through shell history, logs, inherited environments, or host configuration if handled carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files clearly require and document a real API key. The context is legitimate service authentication, but the exposure paths are common operational risks.

low_findings.title: Host Environment File Guidance Is Configuration Documentation
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged hidden file access because the reference mentions .env paths and OpenClaw host configuration. The file only documents where users may store a key; it does not contain code that reads arbitrary files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are documentation text about host configuration paths. No executable file-read logic or exfiltration path is present in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Heuristic Appears to Be Natural Language Content
low_findings.description: The scanner reported high entropy on the Markdown files. Review found normal Chinese and English documentation with command examples, not encoded payloads, binaries, encrypted blobs, or obfuscated scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Markdown documentation and examples only. No evidence of encoded content or hidden executable payloads was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Documentation directs agents to run rollinggo commands through npx, npm, uvx, uv, and local development commands. These are expected for the skill, but execution should be treated as running third-party code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and repeated across the main skill and references. They are legitimate instructions, but still cross a code execution boundary.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret Access Pattern for RollingGo_API_KEY
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells users and agents to provide RollingGo_API_KEY through environment variables or command arguments. This is necessary for API access, but it should be isolated from logs and unrelated tools.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The secret name and configuration examples are directly present. The context is legitimate authentication, with no evidence that the skill itself steals the key.

content.value_statement: Hotel research often needs current prices, room details, and structured filters. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to use RollingGo CLI for hotel search and comparison.
actual_capabilities: Search hotels by destination, natural language query, date, guest count, distance, budget, and star rating.
Retrieve hotel detail results by hotelId or hotel name when supported by the RollingGo CLI.
Fetch hotel tag lists for preferred, required, or excluded tag filters.
Compare candidate hotels using structured result fields and current room price data.
Guide users to booking URLs or hotel detail links returned by RollingGo results.
limitations: Requires a valid RollingGo_API_KEY or an explicit --api-key value.
Requires npx, npm, uvx, uv, or an installed rollinggo command in the runtime environment.
Only covers hotel search and hotel detail workflows, not flights, trains, car rental, or transfers.
Hotel availability, prices, and booking links depend on the live RollingGo service response.

use_cases.title: Compare Hotels Near a Destination
use_cases.description: Find hotels near a city, landmark, airport, or address, then compare options by price, distance, and rating.

use_cases.title: Filter Hotels by Brand and Amenities
use_cases.description: Use hotel tags and brand filters to narrow options for breakfast, family needs, room size, or preferred chains.

use_cases.title: Check Room Prices Before Booking
use_cases.description: Look up room details and current rates for a specific hotel after choosing a candidate from search results.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Hotel Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search by place and date
prompt_templates.prompt: Search hotels near Tokyo Disney for two adults from April 1 to April 3, 2026. Show the best five options with price and distance.

prompt_templates.title: Budget and Star Rating Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find four or five star hotels near Shanghai Disney for two nights under 800 CNY per night. Include hotelId values for follow-up checks.

prompt_templates.title: Tag-Based Hotel Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the hotel tag list, then search for family friendly hotels near Tokyo Disney that include breakfast. Use exact tag names from RollingGo.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Hotel Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Search three candidate hotels near Ginza, fetch room details for each hotelId, and compare refundable rooms, prices, room size, and booking links.

output_examples.input: Search hotels near Shanghai Disney for two adults, April 1 to April 3, with a nightly budget under 800 CNY.
output: A ranked shortlist of hotels near Shanghai Disney.
Each result includes name, hotelId, approximate distance, star rating, nightly price, and a short comparison note.
The response highlights which options need a follow-up room detail check.

output_examples.input: Find hotels near Tokyo Disney that include breakfast and are family friendly.
output: The assistant first retrieves valid RollingGo hotel tags.
The final results use exact tag names and explain how each hotel matches the requested amenities.
Hotels without matching tags are excluded or marked as weaker matches.

output_examples.input: Check room prices for hotelId 123456 for two adults and one room.
output: A room-level summary with available room names, current prices, cancellation notes when present, and booking links.
The answer avoids inventing unavailable room details and reports when the live API returns no rooms.

best_practices: Use environment-based API key injection instead of placing secrets directly in prompts or command text.
Run hotel-tags before tag-based searches so filters use exact supported tag strings.
Use hotelId for detail lookups whenever search results provide it.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for flights, trains, car rental, or non-hotel travel products.
Do not assume prices are final until the live room detail or booking page confirms them.
Do not pass --format table to hotel-detail or hotel-tags because those commands expect JSON output.

faq.question: Does this skill book hotels directly?
faq.answer: No. It can return booking URLs or hotel links from RollingGo results, but the user completes booking outside the skill.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: The workflow requires a valid RollingGo_API_KEY or an explicit --api-key argument for RollingGo CLI commands.

faq.question: Can it search by hotel amenities?
faq.answer: Yes. It can fetch hotel tags and use exact tag strings for preferred, required, or excluded tag filters.

faq.question: Which runtimes are supported?
faq.answer: The references cover npm or npx for Node.js and uv or uvx for Python environments.

faq.question: Why does the audit show medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill runs external CLI packages and handles an API key. These are valid actions, but they need controlled execution and secret handling.

faq.question: Can it compare multiple hotels?
faq.answer: Yes. It can search candidate hotels, fetch details by hotelId, and summarize differences in price, distance, tags, and rooms.
